metadata.title: Science Foundations
metadata.description: A starter pack that adds chemistry and biology foundations with a small cross-subject bridge.
metadata.author: Knowledge Nexus

subjects.name: Chemistry
subjects.description: The study of substances, their structure, properties, and transformations.

subjects.name: Biology
subjects.description: The study of living systems, from cells to ecosystems.

nodes.title: Atomic Structure
nodes.subject: chemistry
nodes.description: Atomic structure covers protons, neutrons, electrons, isotopes, and electron arrangement.
nodes.why_it_matters: It explains why elements behave differently and how matter is built.

nodes.title: Chemical Bonding
nodes.subject: chemistry
nodes.description: Chemical bonding explains how atoms form ionic, covalent, and metallic bonds.
nodes.why_it_matters: Bonding determines the structure and behavior of molecules and materials.

nodes.title: Biomolecules
nodes.subject: biology
nodes.description: Biomolecules include carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids.
nodes.why_it_matters: They are the chemical building blocks of cells and life processes.

nodes.title: Cell Structure
nodes.subject: biology
nodes.description: Cells contain membranes, organelles, and internal systems that support life.
nodes.why_it_matters: Cell structure is the foundation for understanding how organisms function.

nodes.title: Molecular Biology
nodes.subject: biology
nodes.description: Molecular biology explores gene expression, protein synthesis, and regulation at the molecular level.
nodes.why_it_matters: It is central to biotechnology, disease research, and modern genetics.

tests.title: Atomic Structure Test
tests.instructions: Test your understanding of atoms and subatomic structure.

questions.prompt: What is the smallest unit of an element that still retains that element's identity?
Which subatomic particle has a positive charge?

tests.title: Cell Structure Test
tests.instructions: Test your understanding of the main parts of a cell.

questions.prompt: Which organelle is often called the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria release usable energy for the cell.

questions.prompt: What structure surrounds the cell and controls what enters and leaves?
questions.explanation: That role belongs to the cell membrane.
